objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 transcript export assessment where a normal teacher export can request sensitive student fields.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
Students, transcripts, guardians, and support fields are intentionally fake.

actions: Identify whether sensitive transcript fields require a privileged role or purpose-specific approval
successCriteria: Export schema and sensitive-field policy evidence are recorded
noSubagents: privacy impact and disclosure wording stay with the primary operator

actions: Request a default teacher transcript export
Record the sensitive fields returned to the normal teacher context
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites both schema policy and sensitive export evidence
noSubagents: final FERPA/privacy remediation wording stays with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic transcript export schema response
evidence.summary: The export schema exposes an include-all mode that adds sensitive student fields without a privileged-role requirement.

evidence.title: Synthetic teacher transcript export with sensitive fields
evidence.summary: A normal teacher-context export with include=all returned accommodations, discipline note, and guardian email fields.

findings.title: Transcript export exposes sensitive student fields to normal teacher context
findings.description: The synthetic transcript export endpoint allows a normal teacher request to add sensitive fields through include=all without purpose-specific authorization or response-field filtering.
findings.impact: Staff accounts with ordinary classroom access could export student-support and guardian data beyond the minimum needed for transcript review, increasing privacy and compliance risk.
findings.remediation: Use explicit response schemas per role and purpose, deny client-selected sensitive fields by default, require privileged approval for support or discipline fields, and audit sensitive export requests.
